revision.text:
[[ja:愛媛縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine |image=File:Ehime gokoku shrine.jpg |location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, Miyuki Town 1-476 |ISO=JP-38 |Rank degrees=33|Rank minutes=51|Rank seconds=14.48 |longitude degrees=132|longitude minutes=46|longitude seconds=25.83 |deity=National martyrs from Ehime Prefecture, contributors to local industry development, members of the Women's Volunteer Corps, totaling 49,727 spirits |Rank=Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine
Old Prefectural Shrine
Special Shrine |founded=Meiji 32 ({{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}) |main hall={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |festival=April 10
October 10 |map=Japan Matsuyama city |prefecture={{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} |coordinates={{Coord|33.85402777777778|N|132.77383333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine''' (|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} located in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. The shrine crest is a mountain cherry blossom. The haiden features a plaque inscribed with "Gokoku" by Prince {{ill|Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|en|Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|qid=Q1030368|lt=Takeda-no-Miya|1=Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu}}, donated during his visit in Showa 28 ({{ill|1953|en|1953|qid=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}). == deity == The shrine enshrines the spirits of those from Ehime Prefecture who died in national service since the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, including military personnel, civilian employees, female students, nurses, telephone operators, members of the National Service Corps, victims of the {{ill|Toyama Maru|en|Toyama Maru|qid=Q2712113|lt=Toyama Maru|1=Toyama Maru}} and {{ill|Tenth Tojo Maru Sinking Incident|ja|第十東予丸沈没事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenth Tojo Maru Sinking Incident|1=sinking of Tōyo Maru No. 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11602422}}, police officers, firefighters, self-defense forces, and other public servants who died in service. It also honors notable local figures such as {{ill|Katō Yoshiaki|en|Katō Yoshiaki|qid=Q1371657|lt=Kato Yoshiaki|1=Katō Yoshiaki}}, {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|qid=Q1377984|lt=Todo Takatora|1=Tōdō Takatora}}, {{ill|Hisamatsu Sadayuki|ja|久松定行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamatsu Sadayuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Date Hidemune|en|Date Hidemune|qid=Q8190896|lt=Date Hidemune|1=Date Hidemune}}, and others who contributed to the development of the region. == History == * Meiji 32 ({{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}): founded as a private shrine in the current location of {{ill|Matsuyama East Police Station|ja|松山東警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama East Police Station|1=Matsuyama East Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529878}}. * Taisho 2 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}}): Moved to {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431089}} due to the construction of {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Showa 14 ({{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}}) April: Designated as Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine by the Interior Minister. Moved to the current location on October 9. * Showa 20 ({{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}) July: Shrine buildings destroyed by war damage. * Showa 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|qid=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}): Reconstruction began. * Showa 30 ({{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}}) October: Completed. * Showa 41 ({{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}}) April 18: Visited by Emperor {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Showa Emperor|1=Hirohito}} and Empress {{ill|Empress Nagako|en|Empress Nagako|qid=Q230697|lt=Empress Kojun|1=Empress Nagako}} during the 17th {{ill|National Tree-Planting Festival|lt=National Tree-Planting Festival|lt=National Tree-Planting ceremony|ja|全国植樹祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Tree-Planting ceremony|qq=}}.{{Cite book |author=Takeshi Hara |title=Complete Record of the Showa Emperor's Imperial Train |publisher=Shinchosha |year=2016-09-30 |page=129 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|qq=}} ''During the {{ill|Allied Occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍占領下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Allied Occupation of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the shrine was renamed "Miyuki Shrine" following the {{ill|Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|qid=Q3029647|lt=Shinto Directive|1=Shinto Directive}}. The original name was restored in September Showa 27 ({{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=1952|1=1952}}) after the {{ill|Treaty of San Francisco|en|Treaty of San Francisco|qid=Q392541|lt=Treaty of San Francisco|1=Treaty of San Francisco}}.'' == annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! ceremony |- | January 1 || {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saiten-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} |- | January 3 || {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} |- | January 11 || New Grain Offering festival |- | February 1 || {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} |- | February 11 || {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念日祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | First Sunday of March || Tea Whisk Pagoda festival |- | April 10 || Spring Memorial Service |- | May 5 || Manyo festival |- | June 30 || {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | August 13-17 || Lantern festival |- | August 15 || {{ill|End of World War II|ja|終戦記念日祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of World War II|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | October 1 || Flower Mound festival |- | October 10 || Autumn Memorial Service |- | November 23 || {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} |- | December 23 || {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor's Birthday|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} |- | December 31 || Ancient God Tablet Burning ceremony, Great Purification, and New Year's Eve festival |- | Every Day || Daily Worship |- | First of Every Month || Monthly Beginning festival, Flower Offering festival |- | Fifteenth of Every Month || Mid-Month festival |- | End of Every Month || Joint Memorial Service |- | First Sunday of Every Month || Tea Offering festival |- | Third Sunday of Every Month || Song Offering festival |} == Access == * {{ill|Iyotetsu|en|Iyotetsu|qid=Q3138970|lt=Iyotetsu|1=Iyotetsu}} City Tram, get off at [[Red Cross Hospital Station]] and walk for 10 minutes. == Surrounding Facilities == * {{ill|Ehime University|en|Ehime University|qid=Q1074112|lt=Ehime University|1=Ehime University}} * {{ill|Matsuyama University|en|Matsuyama University|qid=Q2496089|lt=Matsuyama University|1=Matsuyama University}} * [[Matsuyama Red Cross Hospital]] == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taga Shrine (Matsuyama)]] * [[:en:22nd Infantry Regiment|22nd Infantry Regiment]] == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku.org/ Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Gokoku Shrines|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
